slides.title: Washing more makes you *oilier*.
slides.sub: Oily, shiny skin usually isn't dirty skin. It's skin fighting back against everything you strip off it.

slides.title: Over-washing backfires
slides.body: Strip your skin too often and it *pumps out rebound oil* to recover. Wash twice a day, gently.

slides.title: Drop the stripping toners
slides.body: Alcohol-based toners feel tight and clean, then *trigger more oil* an hour later. Skip them entirely.

slides.title: Skipping moisturizer backfires
slides.body: Leave oily skin bare and it overcompensates. A *light moisturizer* signals your skin to ease off.

slides.title: Sugar raises sebum
slides.body: Big blood-sugar spikes *raise sebum production*. Cutting added sugar visibly drops the midday shine.

slides.title: Stress and sleep drive oil
slides.body: Poor sleep *bumps stress hormones* that crank up oil. Deep sleep keeps your skin calmer and matte.

slides.title: Balanced, matte, *less shine*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food & sleep that quietly drive your oil — connected to one simple daily score.

caption:
If you fight oily skin by washing more, you're feeding the problem. Strip it and it pumps out rebound oil. 🫧

Wash twice gently, ditch harsh toners, moisturize light, cut sugar, sleep deep. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the food & sleep that drive your shine → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Washing Your Face More Makes It Oilier
